user_query:
Act as my PM copilot and produce the first useful artifact for an "Offline Mode" MVP for our mobile notes app. Read all materials under input/ and then:

1) Write a structured briefing to output/pm_pack.json with this schema and rules:
- task_type: set exactly to "PRD drafting".
- optimizing_for: brief one-line statement of what you optimized for.
- feedback_synthesis:
  - theme_counts: object mapping each theme in input/feedback.jsonl to its total count across all rows.
  - top_negative_themes: the top 2 themes by negative sentiment frequency in descending order.
- competitor_insights:
  - competitors: array of competitor names from input/competitors.json.
  - key_gaps: array including every unique string listed under limitations for all competitors in input/competitors.json.
- meeting_decisions: array including all decision bullets from the Decisions section of input/meeting_notes.md.
- open_questions: array including all bullets from the Open Questions section of input/meeting_notes.md.
- prd_outline: { sections: ["Problem","Goals","Non-Goals","User Stories","Requirements","Success Metrics","Open Questions","Timeline","Competitor Comparison","Next Steps"] }
- success_metrics: array including both business goal statements from input/business_goals.yaml.
- recommended_next_step: a concrete next step (string, at least 10 characters).

2) Draft a concise PRD to output/offline_mode_prd.md that includes the following sections as Markdown headings and content:
- ## Problem
- ## Goals
- ## Non-Goals
- ## User Stories (at least 3 bullet points that start with "- As a ")
- ## Requirements with subsections "### MUST", "### SHOULD", and "### COULD" (each with at least one bullet)
- ## Success Metrics (must reference the 8% 90-day retention and 12% mobile WAU improvement targets)
- ## Open Questions (must list all open questions from the meeting notes)
- ## Timeline (must include the exact MVP deadline "2026-06-30")
- ## Competitor Comparison (must reference all competitor names from input/competitors.json)
- ## Next Steps

Use the inputs to ground decisions and trade-offs. Make sure your JSON counts and lists are consistent with the source files, and that the PRD sections explicitly reflect the business goals and constraints.

Files to read under input/:
- input/feedback.jsonl — user feedback labeled by theme and sentiment
- input/meeting_notes.md — alignment meeting decisions and open questions
- input/competitors.json — competitor capabilities and limitations
- input/business_goals.yaml — business goals and constraints

Deliverables:
- output/pm_pack.json (per the schema above)
- output/offline_mode_prd.md (with the specified sections and references)
